note: Masculine -o; feminine -a: "un cafe bueno" / "una comida buena".
Feminine mala. The opposite of bueno: "un dia malo" / "una idea mala".
Ends in -e, so it does not change for gender: "un cafe grande" / "una mesa grande".
Feminine pequeña. Say "peh-KEH-nyo" (ñ = ny). The opposite of grande.
Feminine fea. The opposite of bonito: "un edificio feo" / "una casa fea".
Feminine barata. The opposite of caro.
Agrees in gender and number: muchos (m.) / muchas (f.): "muchos amigos" / "muchas mesas".
Feminine pocas. The opposite of muchos: "pocos dias" / "pocas personas".
An adverb of frequency: "voy a menudo" = I go often.
The opposite of a menudo: "nunca como carne" = I never eat meat.
